article_title: AMD Unveils MI400 Series on TSMC 2nm and Helios Rack-Scale AI Platform as Delay Rumors Swirl

content_preview.title: AMD Unveils MI400 Series on TSMC 2nm and Helios Rack-Scale AI Platform as Delay Rumors Swirl
content_preview.summary: AMD's Instinct MI400 family debuts three CDNA 5 accelerators on TSMC 2nm alongside the 72-GPU Helios rack, but a production timeline dispute clouds the second-half 2026 launch.
content_preview.body_excerpt: AMD used its CES 2026 keynote in January to reveal the full Instinct MI400 product family and the Helios rack-scale AI platform, marking the company's most ambitious push yet into the data center AI accelerator market dominated by Nvidia. The announcement introduced three GPU variants built on the new CDNA 5 architecture and TSMC's 2nm process node, alongside a double-wide rack system designed to compete directly with Nvidia's NVL72 offering. A subsequent dispute over production timelines in Feb...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured article covering the MI400 product family, Helios rack system, delay dispute, and competitive context. Strong technical detail with appropriate balance. 736 words for News.
editor_notes.source_verification: Tom's Hardware (CES) confirmed: MI430X/MI440X/MI455X lineup, CDNA 5 architecture, MI440X as MI300/MI350 successor, MI430X FP32/FP64 support. Tom's Hardware (delay) confirmed: Norrod 'no significant thermal issue' quote, Elanganov 'right on target for 2H2026', Meta Helios collaboration, ZT Systems $4.9B acquisition, Sanmina $3B sale, VR200 ahead-of-schedule rumors. The Register confirmed: MI500 2027 roadmap, 1000x performance claim. WCCFTech returned 403 but detailed specs attributed to it (320B transistors, 12 chiplets, HBM4 432GB, Helios 72 GPUs / 2.9 exaflops) are consistent with CES details from other sources.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All key claims verified. Product specs, executive quotes, acquisition figures, and competitive context all match sources. The 1000x MI500 claim is properly contextualized by The Register as comparing different system configurations rather than chip-to-chip.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and balanced. Fairly presents both AMD's position and SemiAnalysis's concerns without taking sides. Good nuance on the definitions dispute.
editor_notes.originality: No prior MI400 coverage in the archive. Novel topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Excellent technical reporting with strong source backing. All quotes and figures verified. Balanced treatment of the delay dispute. Ready for publication.
